Bas-reliefs illustrate Vishnu’s avatars and mythological battles, underscoring themes of protection, cosmic order, and divine justice. These artistic narratives intertwine Vishnu’s  ...    Though originally a Hindu temple, Angkor Wat later incorporated Buddhist elements as Theravada Buddhism rose to prominence in the region. Buddhist statues and iconography gradually accompanied or replaced some Hindu symbols while retaining the temple’s cosmic structure—the transition highlighting the fluid religious identity of Khmer kingship and the enduring power of Vishnu’s symbolism woven into the temple’s fabric.
